description: College-level calculus course covering limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, and advanced topics to prepare students for AP exams and higher-level mathematics.

topics.name: Evaluating Limits Graphically and Algebraically
topics.description: Techniques for finding limits using graphs, tables, and algebraic manipulation including factoring and conjugate multiplication.

topics.name: Limits (Working with Trigonometry)
topics.description: Evaluating trigonometric limits using special limits such as lim(θ→0) sinθ/θ = 1 and applying trigonometric identities.

topics.name: Limits and Rationalization
topics.description: Using rationalization techniques to evaluate limits involving square roots and radicals.

topics.name: Continuity and Piece-wise functions
topics.description: Analyzing continuity at a point, types of discontinuities, and determining parameters for piecewise function continuity.

topics.name: IVT and Squeeze Theorem
topics.description: Applying the Intermediate Value Theorem for root existence and the Squeeze Theorem for bounding limits.

topics.name: Differentiation (Power Rule and Chain Rule)
topics.description: Applying the power rule for polynomial derivatives and the chain rule for composite functions.

topics.name: Differentiation (Product Rule and Quotient Rule)
topics.description: Using the product rule for multiplying functions and the quotient rule for rational functions.

topics.name: Derivatives of Composite Functions
topics.description: Advanced applications of the chain rule for complex nested functions, exponentials, and logarithms.

topics.name: Implicit Differentiation
topics.description: Differentiating equations where y is not isolated, finding dy/dx, and determining tangent lines for curves.

topics.name: Derivatives of Inverse Functions
topics.description: Finding derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions and using the inverse function theorem.

topics.name: Optimization Problems
topics.description: Solving applied maximum/minimum problems using derivatives to optimize area, volume, cost, and other real-world quantities.

topics.name: Concavity and Points of Inflection
topics.description: Determining concavity using second derivatives, finding inflection points, and sketching curves.

topics.name: Related Rates
topics.description: Solving problems involving rates of change of related variables using implicit differentiation with respect to time.

topics.name: Definite and Indefinite Integrals
topics.description: Basic integration techniques, antiderivatives, and evaluating definite integrals geometrically and numerically.

topics.name: Approximating Area with Riemann Sums
topics.description: Using left, right, midpoint, and upper/lower Riemann sums to approximate definite integrals.

topics.name: Trapezoidal Rule and Approximations
topics.description: Approximating definite integrals using trapezoids and analyzing error bounds.

topics.name: Substitution Method
topics.description: Using u-substitution to evaluate indefinite and definite integrals.

topics.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
topics.description: Connecting differentiation and integration, evaluating definite integrals using antiderivatives, and interpreting FTC graphically.

topics.name: Change Theorem
topics.description: Applying the net change theorem to find accumulated change in position, velocity, and other quantities.

topics.name: Introduction to Differential Equations
topics.description: Basic differential equations, slope fields, separation of variables, and modeling exponential growth and decay.

topics.name: Volumes of Solids
topics.description: Finding volumes using disk, washer, and shell methods for solids of revolution and cross-sectional areas.
